Sulphur and element E are elements that placed in the same period in the Periodic Table. F Element F does not belong to any group in the periodic table G Atomic number of element G is 2 H An atom of element H can form a stable ion by losing 2 electrons. The stable ion formed by element H and an atom of element B have the same electronic arrangement I An atom of element I can form a stable ion by gaining 3 electrons. stem_en: 127 3. In 1911, Ernest Rutherford and his coworkers performed the gold foil scattering experiment. In the experiment, they bombarded very thin sheets of gold foil with fast moving alpha particles. Alpha particles can be represented by the symbol 4He2+. From the experimental result, most of the alpha particles passed through without deflection. However, a very small percentage bounced off at very large angles. Rutherford suggested that the atom has a very small positively charged region with a dense mass at the center. (a) Explain why only a very small percentage of alpha particles were deflected and bounced off at very large angles.
